market.wager_status: No confirmed wager was recorded for this matchup

halftime_read.chicago_shooting: 60.0% FG and 40.0% from three created clear regression risk
halftime_read.seattle_scoring_support: Seattle produced 44 despite 18.2% from three and 50.0% FT, supported by 28 paint points, 10 offensive rebounds, and 40 field-goal attempts
halftime_read.structural_signal: The scoring environment was not driven solely by unsustainable perimeter shooting; Seattle's rim pressure, offensive rebounding and possession volume created a durable over path
halftime_read.best_decision: Pass on halftime under 182.5; lean over only at small stake because Chicago regression and late-game execution risk limited the margin

what_decided_game_total: Seattle scored 90 while making only four threes, proving the total was carried by interior volume rather than three-point variance alone
Seattle generated 56 paint points, 16 offensive rebounds, 16 second-chance points and 17 fast-break points
Chicago's first-half 60.0% shooting regressed to 35.9% in the second half, but Seattle answered with 46 second-half points
The fourth quarter remained competitive enough to preserve starter minutes and late fouling, producing 39 points
Chicago's 27 assists on 35 made field goals reflected strong ball movement and repeatable shot creation
model_lessons: Do not auto-fade a 100-point first half when the trailing team has strong rim and offensive-rebound indicators
Separate shooting regression from possession and shot-quality regression
Low three-point percentage can be an over-positive indicator when paint scoring and offensive rebounding are already carrying the offense
At halftime, weight offensive rebounds, paint attempts, free-throw opportunities and game competitiveness alongside raw field-goal percentage
A halftime under needs multiple confirming brakes: declining shot volume, weak rim access, low foul pressure, slower substitutions or a widening blowout

summary: Chicago won 95-90. The pregame over 171 cleared by 14 and the halftime over 182.5 cleared by 2.5. Chicago's extreme first-half shooting did regress, but Seattle's paint scoring, offensive rebounding and sustained possession volume kept the game above the live number. The clean model lesson is to avoid reflexively attacking a halftime under when the apparent shooting inflation is paired with durable interior and possession-based scoring support.
